trigger: Mukul says 'check your work before calling it done' on a UI rendering fix
rule: BEFORE saying a UI fix is done, run a Playwright measurement (not just a screenshot) that asserts the actual scrollWidth ≤ offsetWidth for every text element you touched. A screenshot at thumbnail resolution can hide truncation; offsetWidth/scrollWidth comparison cannot. Pattern: page.$$eval the elements, return {text, offsetW, scrollW, clipped: scrollW > offsetW}, log it, fix until no element has clipped:true. Then screenshot for visual confirmation. The CLAUDE.md rule 'validate end-to-end before declaring done' applies to UI rendering: type-check ≠ rendered correctly.
why: On 2026-05-22 the dashboard pill column was 42px wide, clipping 'workspace:117' to 'WORKS'. I called the fix done after one round of edits without measuring. Mukul: 'I think the pill shape around "works" is still too tight. Check your work before calling it done.' Re-fix required widening to 130px AND switching display from inline-flex to inline-block (text-overflow doesn't behave on flex items).

sources.user_correction: On 2026-05-22 the dashboard pill column was 42px wide, clipping 'workspace:117' to 'WORKS'. I called the fix done after one round of edits without measuring. Mukul: 'I think the pill shape around "works" is still too tight. Check your work before calling it done.' Re-fix required widening to 130px AND switching display from inline-flex to inline-block (text-overflow doesn't behave on flex items).
